quiz.title: Why? / Alice in Wonderland - Unit 4 Quiz

questions.questionText: The poem 'Why?' is about a child who:
options: Asks many questions about the world
questions.explanation: The poem is about a curious child who asks many 'why' questions to understand the world around them.
hints: The title is a question word

questions.questionText: In 'Alice in Wonderland', Alice fell into:
questions.explanation: Alice followed a white rabbit and fell down a rabbit hole into Wonderland.
'What' is a question word (also called WH-word). Other question words include Who, Where, When, Why, How.

questions.questionText: The White Rabbit in Alice in Wonderland was always:
questions.explanation: The White Rabbit was always in a hurry, checking his watch and saying he was late.
hints: He kept looking at his watch

questions.questionText: Which question word asks about a person?
questions.explanation: 'Who' is used to ask about a person. Example: 'Who is your teacher?'

questions.questionText: Which question word asks about time?
questions.explanation: 'When' is used to ask about time. Example: 'When is your birthday?'
hints: It's about hours, days, or years

questions.questionText: True or False: Wonderland was a normal, ordinary place.
questions.explanation: False! Wonderland was a magical, strange place where unusual things happened - animals talked, things changed size.
hints: Think about all the strange things Alice saw

questions.questionText: Complete: '___ is the capital of India?'
questions.explanation: 'What' asks about things or facts. 'What is the capital of India?' asks for a fact (Delhi).
hints: We're asking about a thing, not a person or time

questions.questionText: Why is it good to ask questions?
options: It helps us learn and understand
It's not good to ask questions
questions.explanation: Asking questions helps us learn new things and understand the world better. Curiosity leads to knowledge!
hints: Think about what happens when you ask and get answers

questions.questionText: Which question word asks about a place?
questions.explanation: 'Where' is used to ask about places. Example: 'Where do you live?'
